mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual condition signals sustained headwinds to S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=4.33 near threshold, CPIAUCSL=322.132 elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 58% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector health. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, aggregate demand weakness emerges that depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% MoM AND RSAFS declines > 2% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.5 indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the index is above trend.
failure_condition: Periods with INDPRO > 104.5 do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 54% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market slack that triggers Fed rate-cut expectations within 60 trading days. Equity investors front-run this monetary easing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates compress in anticipation.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the lead-lag relationship between UNRATE and forward returns reverses sign in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) creates a regime where the Fed is tightening into growth, signaling policy error risk. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession probability rises.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerating below 3% year-over-year signals weakening consumer demand that compresses forward earnings estimates. This threshold-triggered condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity analysts revise guidance downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.3%) signals simultaneous supply and demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk crystallizes in equity pricing.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² increase < 0.02) over single-variable models.
